business.headline: SBI Funds Management IPO GMP hints at 16% listing gains, but should you subscribe?SBI Funds Management is India's largest asset management company (AMC) and manages the SBI Mutual Fund. It is a joint venture between State Bank of India and French asset manager Amundi.

Trump wants a 20% Hormuz toll. What it could mean for India's fuel billIndia is in a better position to weather a disruption than it was a few months ago, but any prolonged instability around Hormuz could still push up oil prices, freight costs and inflation.

EPFO's plan to raise wage ceiling to Rs 25,000 put on hold. Here's whyAt present, the statutory wage ceiling for mandatory contributions under the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF) and Employees' Pension Scheme (EPS) is Rs 15,000 a month. The limit has remained unchanged since 2014.
Sensex, Nifty open lower as oil prices jump on fresh US-Iran tensionsThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 314.58 points to 77,301.82, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 85.45 points to 24,125.55 as of 9:35 am.

Nifty at 26,500? Goldman Sachs says these stocks, sectors could lead the rallyGoldman Sachs expects the benchmark index to reach 26,500, implying an upside of around 9.5% from Monday's closing level of 24,211. But more importantly for investors, it has identified the sectors and stocks it believes are best placed to benefit if the market recovery gathers pace.

SBI Funds Management IPO opens today: Should you subscribe? Check GMP, detailsThe SBI Funds Management IPO opens for subscription on July 14, 2026, and closes on July 16, 2026. The IPO is entirely an Offer for Sale (OFS), which means the company will not receive any money from the issue. Instead, the existing shareholders are selling part of their stake.

Why HDFC Bank reduced its workforce by over 3,300 despite growing businessThe annual report shows that HDFC Bank's total employee strength stood at 2,11,178 as of March 31, 2026, down by 3,343 employees from the previous year

Russia, Iran, North Korea moved $104 billion in crypto to bypass sanctions: ReportChainalysis says Russia, Iran and North Korea used cryptocurrencies to keep funds moving under sanctions in 2025. The report points to a fast-growing digital route for sanctions evasion, theft and restricted transactions.

BSNL's core services revenue rises 10% in Q1FY27 as govt pushes for its turnaroundBSNL's core services revenue rose to Rs 4,418 crore in Q1FY27 from 4,017 crore in the corresponding quarter last year, driven largely by growth in its Enterprise Business and Consumer Mobility segments.

India-US trade deal framework ready as talks progress amid tariff uncertaintyIn February, the two sides agreed to 18% tariffs on Indian goods in exchange for New Delhi lowering trade barriers and buying more American goods. But a final deal has been clouded by the U.S. Supreme Court ruling invalidating President Donald Trump's sweeping global tariffs.

About 2 crore ITRs filed so far. Should you wait for a deadline extension?With around two crore returns already filed and the filing process running smoothly this year, taxpayers may be better off completing their returns well before the deadline.

From Rs 5,000 late fee to 200% penalty: Income tax mistakes that could cost you bigA small mistake in your income tax compliance can turn into a big financial setback. From late filing fees to steep penalties for under-reporting income, the Income Tax Act prescribes strict consequences for different types of defaults. Are you aware of the most common ones?

June retail inflation climbs to 4.38%, breaches RBI's 4% target after 17 monthsThe latest reading marks the first time in 17 months that headline inflation has crossed the RBI's 4% target. Retail inflation had eased to 2.74% in January before gradually rising to 3.21% in February, 3.40% in March, 3.48% in April and 3.93% in May.

Piyush Goyal dismisses report claiming India is holding back on US trade dealThe rebuttal comes at a sensitive stage in India-US trade negotiations, with both sides racing to bridge differences on tariffs and market access ahead of a proposed bilateral trade agreement.

Why SK Hynix shares crashed over 15% days after their Nasdaq debutJust days after making a strong start in the US market, the South Korean chipmaker's shares plunged more than 15% on Monday, as investors rushed to book profits.

Delhi's property boom is creating an unusual courtroom problemWhat do soaring home prices have to do with courtrooms? According to the Delhi High Court, more than you might think. Here's the unusual problem now under scrutiny.

Why is crude oil back on the boil?Higher crude oil prices increase the country's import bill and can put pressure on inflation if fuel and transportation costs rise. They can also weaken the rupee as oil marketing companies require more US dollars to pay for imports.

Why TCS, Infosys and HCLTech shares rose despite a weak marketShares of Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), Infosys, HCLTech and LTIMindtree rose in early trade, outperforming the broader market and signalling renewed investor interest in a sector that has been under pressure for months.

What's behind India's tougher stance in trade talks with USThe US entered trade talks from a position of strength. But a series of developments may be tilting the balance towards New Delhi. Here's why India is in no hurry to strike a deal.

Sensex falls over 300 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Indian benchmark indices fell sharply after renewed US-Iran hostilities lifted crude oil concerns. The sell-off reflected worries over India's import bill, inflation and the market impact if Brent climbs above $90.

entertainment.headline: Don't judge my grief by tears you don't see: S Janaki's granddaughter in moving note S Janaki's granddaughter posted a note asking people not to judge her grief by her tears after the singer's death. She said loss is deeply personal, recalled her late sister Varsha, and spoke of the pain of public scrutiny.

I was written off: Govinda announces Roopa, his first film in seven years Bollywood actor Govinda announced his comeback film Roopa, marking his return to the big screen after seven years. The actor reflected on career setbacks and said he hopes the film strikes a chord with young audiences.

Actor Anuj Sachdeva slams Hina, Rubina for remarks on Gaurav Khanna's personal life A discussion on The POV about Gaurav Khanna and Akanksha Chamola's choice not to have children drew sharp attention online. Anuj Sachdev's reaction intensified the row and renewed debate over comments on the couple's private life.

The Vampire Diaries star Paul Wesley marries girlfriend Natalie Kuckenburg Actor Paul Wesley and girlfriend Natalie Kuckenburg have married in a quiet ceremony attended only by a minister and their dog Greg. The couple dated for several years and got engaged in 2025.

Anurag Kashyap watches Mukundan Unni Associates, calls it absolute banger Anurag Kashyap watched Mukundan Unni Associates with director Abhinav Sunder Nayak and praised it warmly. His signed note, Letterboxd review and Vineeth Sreenivasan's response amplified the film's buzz.

Alia Bhatt joins Tumbbad 2 with Sohum Shah and Nawazuddin Siddiqui Alia Bhatt has confirmed she will star in Tumbbad 2 alongside Sohum Shah and Nawazuddin Siddiqui. Her announcement ends weeks of speculation and reflects the original film's lasting pull on audiences.

Don't let Phunsukh Wangdu die: 3 Idiots' Chatur appeals amid Wangchuk hunger strike Actor Omi Vaidya urged people to pay attention to Sonam Wangchuk's indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar. His appeal came as concern deepened over Wangchuk's worsening health and the protest's demands.

Mumbai cafe manager had no clue he was serving Nolan, 'Spider-Man'; googled later Tom Holland, Matt Damon and Christopher Nolan stopped at Mumbai's Olympia Coffee House before The Odyssey premiere. After the brief chai break went viral, the cafe manager said he recognised Holland only after Googling him.

Rihanna makes surprise stage comeback at Jay-Z's New York show, says 'I'm rusty' Rihanna made a surprise appearance at Jay-Z's concert at Yankee Stadium on Monday. This marked her first live performance since the Ambani pre-wedding celebrations in India in 2024.

Lagaan turns 25: Aamir Khan reunites with Rachel Shelley at London Indian film fest Actors Aamir Khan and Rachel Shelley came together at the London Indian Film Festival to mark 25 years of Lagaan. The reunion and fans' nostalgic responses underlined the film's enduring place in popular memory.

Hadd mein rehna: Zareen Khan slams paparazzi over inappropriate comment Actor Zareen Khan recently rebuked paparazzi at a store launch after a remark upset her. The viral exchange renewed attention on actors pushing back against intrusive behaviour.

Why Surbhi Jyoti's daughter's name Seher will ring a bell for Qubool Hai fans Surbhi Jyoti's latest Instagram post has given Qubool Hai fans a nostalgic surprise. The television actor finally revealed the name of her month-old daughter on Monday, and it is one that long-time viewers of the hit show will instantly recognise.

Dhamaal 4 box office Day 4: Fantastic Monday for Ajay Devgn film, tops Rs 100 crore Actor Ajay Devgn's Dhamaal 4 crossed Rs 100 crore worldwide on its fourth day in theatres. The Monday figures kept it marginally ahead of recent comedy releases and extended the franchise's strongest opening run.

Kim Kardashian's 'favourite people' photo dump features Lewis Hamilton, her kids Socialite Kim Kardashian shared photos from her Fourth of July lakeside holiday with her children and Lewis Hamilton. The Instagram post offered a glimpse of family moments, festive celebrations and a cosy selfie with the F1 champion.

We respect each other: Anupam Kher on Naseeruddin Shah's resurfaced 'clown' remark Actor Anupam Kher has reacted to Naseeruddin Shah's resurfaced "clown" remark, saying the two actors share mutual respect despite their past differences. He also claimed the old video was revived to target him after his recent comments on the Ram Temple.

Korean Kanakaraju teaser: Possessed Varun Tej takes on Korean mafia The makers of Korean Kanakaraju have unveiled the official teaser and fixed its theatrical release for August 7. The glimpse sets up Varun Tej in two contrasting shades while linking Rayalaseema, the supernatural elements and Korean gangster lore.

Lock Upp's Akanksha Chamola, earlier identified as bisexual, now says she's asexual Lock Upp contestant Akanksha Chamola, who earlier revealed she was bisexual before marriage, has now said she identifies as asexual. The actor explained that sexuality can evolve with different phases of life and linked her current feelings to her ongoing divorce.

Amitabh Bachchan says unfair decisions at FIFA World Cup 2026 leave him distressed Amitabh Bachchan said some decisions at the 2026 FIFA World Cup left him "distressed" as he shared his thoughts on the tournament in his latest blog. The actor also reflected on national pride, teamwork and the changing times.

Avengers Doomsday concept art: First look at RDJ's Doctor Doom, Loki and Beast Former Marvel visual development director Andy Park has shared Avengers: Doomsday concept art showing Robert Downey Jr's Doctor Doom mask, Loki and the returning X-Men. The artwork marks Park's final full film at Marvel Studios and offers an early look at characters not yet seen in promotional material.

general news.headline: Government summons Iranian diplomats after Indian sailor killed in Hormuz strikesThe move came a day after the UAE said Iranian cruise missiles struck the tankers Mombasa and Al Bahiyah in the southern shipping lane of the Strait of Hormuz, within Omani waters. The attack killed one Indian crew member and injured eight others.

Average car owner can't check mileage on their own: Nitin Gadkari amid E20 rowThe minister said car owners cannot accurately measure mileage themselves and should depend on readings taken at authorised dealers. His remarks came amid growing complaints over the government's ethanol blending mandate.

Meet the IAS officer leading Maharashtra's toughest food safety crackdownMaharashtra's FDA has launched a statewide drive against milk adulteration under commissioner Tukaram Mundhe. The action has exposed synthetic milk units, disrupted supplies and sparked calls for compliance time.
Why Gadkari is on the road, Puri in blended oilNitin Gadkari is back monitoring highways and Hardeep Singh Puri is defending the ethanol policy. What's behind the reshuffle that's confusing people?

Why the football world has turned against ArgentinaFIFA World Cup 2026: Argentina's rise from Messi's tragic nearly-men to world champions has changed how football fans view them. Their success now attracts suspicion, backlash and scrutiny that often follows title-winning sides.

CBSE makes third language internal assessment mandatory for Class 9, 10 studentsCBSE has made the third language internal assessment mandatory for Class 9 and 10 students under the NEP 2020 framework. From the 2027-28 academic session, students will have to clear the school-based assessment to qualify for the Class 10 pass certificate.

Don't let Phunsukh Wangdu die: 3 Idiots' Chatur appeals amid Wangchuk hunger strikeActor Omi Vaidya urged people to pay attention to Sonam Wangchuk's indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar. His appeal came as concern deepened over Wangchuk's worsening health and the protest's demands.

Trump wants a 20% Hormuz toll. What it could mean for India's fuel billIndia is in a better position to weather a disruption than it was a few months ago, but any prolonged instability around Hormuz could still push up oil prices, freight costs and inflation.

What's kharku in Satluj? Why Khalistanis beheaded AIR Patiala station directorThe use of the word "kharku" for Khalistani terrorists in Diljit Dosanjh's Satluj has reignited a debate over the vocabulary used in the film now taken down by ZEE5. What does the word mean? How's it linked to the beheading of ML Manchanda, the station director of AIR Patiala, by Babbar Khalsa?

Video: Family chased for 15 km, hit with rods for objecting to harassmentAccording to the family, they had visited the Bhavali waterfall when they noticed a group of men harassing a young woman. When they intervened and questioned their behaviour, the accused turned violent.

How Sourav Joshi made his Merc. YouTuber's rags-to-crorepati story not fuelled by E20A decade ago, Sourav Joshi watched luxury cars roll into other people's driveways while helping his father at construction sites. Today, the Mercedes he drives has sparked a row after India's most popular YouTuber raised concerns over E20-blended petrol. This is what his journey was powered by. Certainly not E20.

Elephant kills four members of same Nepal family in 14 yearsFour members of the same Nepal family were killed by a rogue elephant over 14 years, even after the family moved away hoping to escape the animal's deadly path.

On camera, drunk tourists from Delhi rain punches, kicks on locals in UttarakhandUttarakhand Police arrested six drunk tourists from Rishikesh's Tapovan who were accused of beating up locals after rushing the injured to hospital.

American detained by India's border force an ex-Special Services officer?A 36-year-old American who claimed to have entered India by sea after losing his passport in Thailand was arrested by the Sashastra Seem Bal near the Indo-Nepal border while allegedly trying to cross into Nepal without valid travel documents. Reports suggest he claims to be a former US Special Services officer.

Sonam Wangchuk's 16-day fast: What happens to the human body at every stageSonam Wangchuk completes 16 days without food, doctors explain how the human body shifts from burning sugar to fat and why the health risks rise sharply after two weeks.

Iran's weapons of mass distractionTop US officials believe that the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran has declared closed, is heavily mined. No vessel has hit a mine as of date, leading to the question if the mines were laid in the first place.

IIT dream ends, tier-3 college success begins. Man shares friend's inspiring storyAn X user shared how a close friend missed IIT and later excelled at their engineering college. The post sparked reactions about mindset, initiative and making the most of available opportunities.

Maharashtra's Ladki Bahin scheme faces fresh heat as women allege payments stoppedWith Rakhi approaching, thousands of women say the Rs 1,500 they depended on has suddenly stopped. As the government cites verification, a CAG report and ground reality raise uncomfortable questions.

No paid crowd: PM's Melbourne event organisers seek Rahul Gandhi, Kharge apology'Melbourne Meets Modi' event organisers rejected Congress leaders' claims that attendees were a 'paid crowd', asserting that the nearly 30,000 participants attended voluntarily at their own or community-supported expense.

What is Pickaxe Mountain, Iran's secretive nuclear site in Trump's sights?Trump's warning has renewed focus on Pickaxe Mountain, a heavily fortified underground complex that experts say could lie beyond the reach of US bunker-buster bombs. Satellite imagery has shown continued activity there, keeping alive questions over Iran's nuclear programme despite last year's strikes.

Man quits corporate career after 18 years, says office politics cost him promotionDharmendra Pandey, who describes himself in his Instagram bio as someone "helping working professionals build an income outside their job," posted a video reflecting on his nearly two decades in the corporate world and the lessons he learnt before quitting.

Railways land equal to 42 Narendra Modi cricket stadiums under encroachment: RTIAn RTI response from the Railway Board has revealed the scale of Indian Railways' encroachment challenge: 1,068.54 hectares of its land under illegal occupation as of March 2025. The figures reveal a worrying trend: encroachments have grown over the years and the Railways lacks a comprehensive long-term database of the problem.

53% of NDA voters don't prefer E20 petrol, 50% fear vehicle damage: C-Voter surveyA C-Voter survey finds more than half of NDA voters unwilling to use E20 petrol, with widespread fears over vehicle damage and lower mileage. The findings reveal why the Centre's ethanol push continues to face resistance.

UAE tankers ignored warnings: Iran on Hormuz strikes that killed Indian sailorThe response by Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) came after the United Arab Emirates confirmed that the attack in Omani territorial waters had killed an Indian crew member and injured eight others.

sports.headline: Mbappe cleared fit as France coach plays mind games before semifinal vs Spain FIFA World Cup: Kylian Mbappe has been declared fully fit to lead France, but manager Didier Deschamps wasted no time playing mind games by labeling Spain the clear favourites ahead of their blockbuster semifinal in Dallas.

Coaching England, Brendon McCullum roots for Virat Kohli in ODI series vs India England white-ball coach Brendon McCullum lavished praise on Virat Kohli ahead of the ODI series against India, calling him one of cricket's all-time greats and hoping the former India captain enjoys a successful campaign.

Draped in diamonds, birthday boy Yamal delivers strong message against racism FIFA World Cup 2026: Sporting a stunning self-gifted diamond necklace on his 19th birthday, Spain prodigy Lamine Yamal lit up Dallas with a powerful message of integration and unity ahead of a highly anticipated clash against France.

France vs Spain: Mbappe and Yamal headline World Cup semi-final showdown FIFA World Cup 2026: France and Spain meet in the World Cup semi-final in Dallas after contrasting runs to the last four. The tie pits Mbappe's direct attacking threat against Spain's control, with a place in the final at stake.

Harry Kane blames English mentality for rumours of rift before Argentina semifinal FIFA World Cup: Harry Kane has dismissed suggestions of a rift between Thomas Tuchel and the England squad following Jude Bellingham's emotional post-match remarks after the quarter-final win over Norway, insisting the camp remains united as focus shifts to the World Cup semi-final against Argentina.

PV Sindhu, Lakshya Sen and Sat-Chi begin tune-up for World Championships PV Sindhu and Sat-Chi lead India's Japan Open campaign in Tokyo. The Super 750 offers World Championships preparation as Sat-Chi return from injury and India chase a first elusive title.

1st ODI preview: India rely on comeback men Rohit, Virat to lift dampened spirits England vs India, 1st ODI preview: India will look to seal their first win on the tour after a disastrous T20I series in England. The return of Virat Kohli, Rohit Sharma and Jasprit Bumrah adds firepower to the side, led by Shubman Gill.

Why the football world has turned against Argentina FIFA World Cup 2026: Argentina's rise from Messi's tragic nearly-men to world champions has changed how football fans view them. Their success now attracts suspicion, backlash and scrutiny that often follows title-winning sides.

Arshad Nadeem confirms participation in CWG 2026, set to face Neeraj Chopra The Neeraj Chopra-Arshad Nadeem rivalry is set for another chapter after the Pakistan star confirmed his participation in the Commonwealth Games 2026. Nadeem will defend the gold medal he won in Birmingham, while Chopra returns to the Games after missing the previous edition through injury.

RCB coach Andy Flower will not leave IPL to join England Test team: Karthik RCB head coach Andy Flower is one of the early favourites to become the head coach of the England Test team. However, former India cricketer, Dinesh Karthik believes that there is one small problem which would stop the coach from switching sides to England.

Brendon McCullum breaks silence after sacking from England Test coach role Brendon McCullum said he was disappointed after losing the England Test job and apologised to supporters. He said that Bazball changed the side's identity, but failures in the Ashes and against India proved decisive.

Harmanpreet calls for more women's Test matches after historic win at Lord'sIndia captain Harmanpreet Kaur has called for more women's Test matches after leading her side to a historic 270-run victory over England at Lord's. Speaking after India's massive win, she hoped that the team gets "ten more Test matches" to continue proving themselves in the longest format.

Loved Sooryavanshi's Wimbledon look? Decoding his Centre Court style gameLiked Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's sharp look at the All England Club? From his structured black suit and striped club tie to those sleek leather loafers, we decode the teenage batting prodigy's effortless, last-minute Wimbledon style game.

Retirement far off, Kohli helping India plan for ODI World Cup 2027India captain Shubman Gill has revealed that former India captain Virat Kohli is heavily involved in the planning of ODI World Cup 2027. Gill also answered queries about Rohit Sharma and his place in the team going into the marquee tournament.

History at Home of Cricket: India win first-ever women's Test at Lord'sIt took more than 140 years for Lord's to host its first-ever women's Test. India made the wait worthwhile, hammering England by 270 runs. Yastika Bhatia's century, Smriti Mandhana's twin fifties and Kranti Gaud's five-wicket haul powered a landmark victory at the Home of Cricket.

Sachin drops in at Lord's, leads pep talk as India women chase historic winSachin Tendulkar paid a surprise visit to the Indian women's team at Lord's ahead of the fourth day of the historic one-off Test against England. He was seen leading the talk to the team as Harmanpreet Kaur's side closed in on a famous victory.

Day 4 Highlights: India win by 270 runs, win first-ever women's Test at Lord'sEngland (ENG) vs India (IND) Day 4 Highlights, women's one-off Test: Harmanpreet Kaur's India win the first ever women's Test match at Lord's. India beat England by a massive margin of 270 runs.

After dashing Wimbledon appearance, Sanju Samson reveals Novak Djokovic inspirationAt Wimbledon, Sanju Samson said Novak Djokovic's discipline and demeanour remain a benchmark for athletes. Yuvraj Singh backed that view, highlighting Djokovic's self-belief, longevity and influence on younger players across the globe.

Australian cricket rocked by cheating scandal days after T20 Women's World Cup winAustralia women's cricket has been engulfed in controversy after Ashleigh Gardner's estranged wife, Monica Wright, publicly accused the all-rounder of having an affair with teammate Georgia Voll. Here's everything we know.

How Sachin Tendulkar is backing a quiet sporting revolution in BastarSachin Tendulkar visited Dantewada to support Bastar's Maidan Cup and meet village children. The initiative is expanding community playgrounds and restoring confidence through sport in a region shaped by insurgency.

Shreyas Iyer's interview over Sooryavanshi snub irks ex-captain: Not the way to talkKris Srikkanth criticised Shreyas Iyer and the team management for dropping Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in Southampton T20I. He said the move and Iyer's remarks showed poor handling of a young player.

IPL: Stephen Fleming steps down as CSK head coach after 17 years at helmIPL: Chennai Super Kings parted ways with head coach Stephen Fleming after an 18-year association, drawing the curtains on the IPL's most successful coaching era. CSK finished three consecutive seasons outside the top four.

India's T20I freefall explained: Did Gambhir and Agarkar fix what wasn't broken?India's disastrous 0-6 tour of Ireland and England has raised questions far bigger than Shreyas Iyer's winless start as T20I captain. In trying to rebuild a world champion side, have Gautam Gambhir and the selection committee dismantled a setup that never really needed fixing?

Andy Flower took England to No.1: Nasser backs RCB coach to replace McCullumFormer England captain Nasser Hussain has backed Andy Flower to replace Brendon McCullum as England's Test head coach, saying his meticulous approach is exactly what the team needs after the Bazball era.

Zverev aims to move beyond tennis' third guy tag, close gap on Sinner and AlcarazWimbledon runner-up Alexander Zverev will return to world No. 2 on Monday, but believes his attacking game has finally brought him closer to Jannik Sinner and Carlos Alcaraz after years of being labelled tennis' "third guy".

Not at full throttle, Jannik Sinner still stands atop the tennis worldJannik Sinner proved that true champions are not defined only by dominance but by their ability to survive adversity and find a way to win. On Sunday, the Italian showcased exactly that, defending his Wimbledon title with a hard-fought victory over Alexander Zverev in the final.

Yastika Bhatia reveals how Mandhana's advice fuelled her Lord's comeback centuryINDW vs ENGW, one-off Test: Yastika Bhatia credited Smriti Mandhana's advice for helping her overcome a serious knee injury after marking her comeback with a maiden international century at Lord's.

The Bazball verdict: Brilliant at home, exposed by the eliteBrendon McCullum's Bazball promised to revolutionise England's Test cricket. Four years later, sacked after a humiliating Ashes defeat, the numbers reveal the truth: it worked at home and against weak teams. Against India and Australia, it crumbled.

Lord's Test: Yastika Bhatia, Kranti Gaud leave India on verge of famous win at Day 3Yastika Bhatia became the first woman to score a Test hundred at Lord's before Kranti Gaud's fiery spell left England six down as India took complete control on Day 3 of the historic Women's Test, setting the hosts a daunting 457-run target.

Alexander Zverev's runner-up finish fuels belief he can one day win WimbledonAlexander Zverev said reaching his maiden Wimbledon final has convinced him he can one day lift the title despite losing to Jannik Sinner. The German praised the world No. 1 while reflecting on a breakthrough fortnight at the All England Club.

Ice-cool Jannik Sinner fights off feisty Alexander Zverev to retain crownWorld No.1 Jannik Sinner came from a set down to beat Alexander Zverev 6-7(7), 7-6(2), 6-3, 6-4 and successfully defend his Wimbledon title on Sunday, July 12 on Centre Court.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
